title: Sustainable Architecture
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The principle of (0) sustainability is now central to modern architectural design and urban planning. Architects are increasingly focused on creating buildings that are (17) environmentally friendly and energy-efficient. The use of (18) renewable materials such as timber and bamboo is becoming more common, as is the (19) installation of solar panels and green roofs.||Such (20) innovative designs can significantly reduce a building's carbon footprint. They can also improve the (21) wellbeing of occupants by providing natural light and better air quality. Critics argue that sustainable building methods can be (22) expensive, though costs are falling as technologies improve. In the long term, the (23) economic benefits of energy-efficient buildings are clear, making sustainability a (24) worthwhile investment for property owners and society alike.

example.text: The principle of (0) SUSTAINABILITY is now central to modern architectural design and urban planning. (SUSTAIN)

questions.explanation: The adverb 'environmentally' is formed from 'environment' via 'environmental' + '-ly'.
The adjective 'renewable' is formed from 'renew' by adding the suffix '-able'.
The noun 'installation' is formed from 'install' by adding the suffix '-ation'.
The adjective 'innovative' is formed from 'innovate' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
The adjective 'expensive' is formed from 'expense' by replacing '-e' with '-ive'.
The adjective 'economic' is formed from 'economy' by replacing '-omy' with '-omic'.
The adjective 'worthwhile' is a compound formed from 'worth' and 'while'.
